purpose: 在已修复的 90/120 交易日数据覆盖上，用新的尾盘趋势 baseline d3+ma20_gap 验证市场环境/择时过滤是否能真正降低结构性回撤。前几轮已经证明：机械止损最多改善约 1.27pp DD，因子组合最多改善约 0.81pp DD；因此本轮只测试何时允许开仓，不继续做相邻因子搜索或止损阈值优化。

latest_market_context.summary: A股收盘大反弹，科技高弹性方向显著修复：沪指重回4000点，创业板指和科创方向强，半导体/存储/PCB/元件领涨，油气/煤炭/白酒/地产/零售偏弱。
latest_market_context.interpretation: 这支持测试市场风险偏好与科技主线强度过滤，但不能把单日行情硬编码为规则。

current_factor_baseline.name: trend_quality_ma20
current_factor_baseline.why_promoted: Only factor combo that fully beat elastic_base on return, r/dd, PF, win rate, and half-window stability.

superseded_baseline.name: elastic_base
superseded_baseline.reason_not_preferred: atr_pct was confirmed as a volatility amplifier, not long-window alpha.

latest_decisions: d3+ma20_gap is the new baseline if this tail-entry trend line remains in the system.
Do not restore V9 total score as the sole buy signal.
Do not keep searching nearby factor combinations for drawdown rescue.
Do not keep tuning mechanical stop-loss thresholds.
Current open question: can market regime / timing decide when this high-beta tail-entry module is allowed to trade?

requested_work.description: Read STRATEGY_WORKLOG.md, run_tail_market_filter_backtest.py, run_tail_factor_combo_long_window.py, run_tail_portfolio_backtest.py, run_factor_research.py, and the three latest result JSONs listed above. Do not rerun data coverage repair.

Create a new standalone script, recommended name run_tail_market_timing_filter.py. Reuse existing factor table, execution price attach, and portfolio simulator patterns. Use kline_count=600 and windows of 90 and 120 trading days.

Fixed stock selection baseline: factor score = z(d3) + z(ma20_gap), with the same basic eligibility filters as the prior long-window tests unless explicitly reported otherwise. Fixed execution: dec_E, max_positions=2. Test position_pct 0.15 and 0.20.

Build point-in-time market environment features using only data visible on or before the entry date. At minimum include CSI300 close vs MA20/MA60, CSI300 ret5/ret20, MA20 slope, MA60 slope, CSI300 drawdown from 20d high, CSI300 intraday/day return if available, market breadth ratio from stock kline_dict, breadth change vs prior day, number/share of stocks above MA20 if feasible, and tech/growth proxy strength if available from existing data. If tech proxy is unavailable, mark it unavailable; do not invent it.

Test single market gates first. Required examples: csi_above_ma20, csi_above_ma60, csi_ret20_positive, csi_ret5_positive, ma20_slope_positive, ma60_slope_positive, breadth >= 45/50/55/60%, breadth improving, csi_drawdown_20d <= 3/5/8%, and avoid days after broad market selloff if measurable.

Test combined gates second. Required named gates: risk_on_loose = csi_above_ma20 OR breadth>=50%; risk_on_core = csi_above_ma20 AND csi_ret20_positive AND breadth>=50%; trend_confirmed = csi_above_ma20 AND ma20_slope_positive; breadth_confirmed = breadth>=55% AND breadth_improving; anti_crash = csi_drawdown_20d<=5% AND breadth>=45%; strong_risk_on = csi_above_ma20 AND csi_above_ma60 AND csi_ret20_positive AND breadth>=55%.

Also test a regime-scaled position model, not only hard gates: full size when risk_on_core passes, half size when risk_on_loose passes, zero otherwise. Compare pp20 scaled model against fixed pp15 and fixed pp20.

For each gate/report row, output pass rate, skipped trade count, trade_count_ratio_vs_baseline, total_return, max_drawdown, return_to_drawdown, PF, win rate, avg/median trade return, worst_trade, best_trade, longest_loss_streak, avg_cash_usage, and max_concurrent_positions.
Do stability splits: 90d full, 120d full, 120d first_half, 120d second_half. A filter that only works in one half must not be promoted.

Trace baseline worst trades: compare worst 10 trades under d3+ma20_gap baseline vs best market gate. Explain whether the gate actually avoided large drawdown clusters or merely skipped both winners and losers.

Promote only if the filter materially improves live usability: target 120d pp20 max_drawdown improves by at least 5pp vs -27.65%, total_return remains positive and preferably >= 10%, PF remains > 1.4, trade_count stays >= 60% of baseline, and both halves remain directionally acceptable. If no gate passes, say clearly that this tail-entry line remains observation-only.

recommended_matrix.stock_pool: CSI300 top 60, consistent with prior repaired long-window tests

questions_for_cc: Can market regime/timing reduce 120d pp20 drawdown from -27.65% by at least 5pp while keeping positive return and PF>1.4?
Which environment feature has the most explanatory power: CSI300 trend, CSI300 momentum, breadth, drawdown avoidance, or combined risk-on?
Does a hard gate outperform a scaled exposure model?
Does pp15 plus market timing become materially more live-friendly than pp20?
Do promoted filters work in both halves, or are they just fitted to one regime?
Which baseline worst trades/clusters are avoided by the best gate?
Should this tail-entry module be allowed into daily scan as an active signal, or remain observation-only?
constraints: No future leakage. Entry-date filters may only use information visible on or before the entry date.
Do not use 2026-06-09 live market data inside the historical backtest unless it naturally belongs to the tested historical window. Today's market context is motivation only.
Do not restore V9 total score as the sole buy signal.
Do not continue optimizing atr_pct or elastic_base variants.
Do not continue optimizing stop-loss thresholds in this task.
If a market feature is unavailable from existing data, mark unavailable and explain; do not fabricate proxies without labeling them.
Do not promote high-return filters with low trade count, one-half-only performance, or performance driven by a few extreme winners.
Write outputs to reports and backtest_queue/done exactly as specified.
done_when: reports contains full JSON and Markdown summary
summary clearly states whether any market timing filter is promoted, whether d3+ma20_gap can become an active daily-scan signal, and what the next task should be
